timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's Hasina coverage (per user instruction). Cluster spec flags this subtopic as politically_sensitive: true, so all entries are deliberately neutrally framed and event-anchored. Scoping note: this subtopic overlaps with c8_a_quota_protests and c2_b_bangladesh_transition; to differentiate, c8_b focuses tightly on Hasina personally and the legal accountability arc — her flight, indictments, conviction, India dispute, and party fate — while skipping protest mechanics (c8_a) and broader transition events like Khaleda Zia's return or Yunus's elections debate (c2_b). Contributing articles from user-provided list: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/5/timeline-sheikh-hasinas-reign-ends-after-15-years ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/5/sheikh-hasina-a-critical-misstep-and-the-end-of-15-years-of-rule ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/5/bangladeshs-sheikh-hasina-forced-to-resign-what-happened-and-whats-next ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/5/world-reacts-to-bangladesh-pm-sheikh-hasinas-removal-from-power ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2024/8/5/bangladesh-protests-live-army-chief-to-address-nation-amid-new-protests (Aug 5 liveblog — primary source for Waker-uz-Zaman address entry) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/longform/2024/8/7/how-bangladeshs-gen-z-protests-brought-down-pm-sheikh-hasina (used for Jul 14 razakar comment confirmation) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/27/once-powerful-student-members-of-hasinas-party-bangladeshs-new-outcastes ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/18/bangladesh-ex-ministers-face-massacre-charges-hasina-probe-deadline-set ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2025/11/17/bangladesh-live-court-verdict-against-toppled-ex-pm-sheikh-hasina-expected ; https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2025/11/18/why-india-likely-wont-return-hasina-to-face-bangladesh-death-penalty ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/11/19/bangladeshs-test-after-hasina-conviction-will-it-repeat-her-mistakes ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/12/19/bangladesh-on-edge-after-killing-of-2024-uprising-activist-triggers-unrest . Additional contributing articles drawn from broader AJ coverage to anchor the accountability arc: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/3/students-renew-bangladesh-protests-call-for-nationwide-civil-disobedience (Aug 3 one-point demand) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/9/bangladesh-taking-steps-to-extradite-former-pm-hasina-from-india ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/7/24/shoot-them-sheikh-hasina-ordered-firing-on-bangladesh-protesters-in-2024 ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/11/14/violence-in-bangladesh-as-toppled-hasinas-party-protests-crackdown-trial . Opinion pieces read for context only (no entries drawn per news-over-opinion preference): https://www.aljazeera.com/opinions/2024/8/7/sheikh-hasinas-downfall-and-exile-are-of-her-own-making ; https://www.aljazeera.com/opinions/2024/8/10/after-hasina-cautious-optimism-for-bangladeshs-future . Article read for context but not made into entry as it concerns post-Hasina governance rather than Hasina's fall arc: https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2025/11/13/have-extrajudicial-killings-continued-under-bangladeshs-yunus-government . Article from non-approved outlet excluded: https://www.ndtv.com/world-news/from-student-uprising-to-death-verdict-a-timeline-of-sheikh-hasinas-fall-9651738 (NDTV not on approved outlet list per project spec) ; https://m.thewire.in/article/south/jaishankar-says-mutuality-of-interests-to-be-pursued-with-bangladesh (The Wire, regional, not on approved list). Date approximations per rule 4: Three same-date entries on Aug 5, 2024 preserved as materially distinct news developments (Hasina's flight, army chief's address, world leaders' reactions). Aug 7 entry (AJ analysis of critical misstep) pegged to publication date of AJ analysis piece. Oct 27 entry pegged to AJ piece on Awami League student-wing members; the social-political phenomenon developed over weeks but Oct 27 is the date AJ codified it as reporting. Nov 18 entry pegged to AJ feature publication date. Nov 19 entry pegged to AJ analysis publication date. Source disagreements: Hasina's 'razakar' framing — Hasina later denied she had insulted protesters and said the term was misinterpreted; AJ entry reports the statement neutrally as it was made July 14. Conviction in absentia (Nov 17, 2025) — the trial's legitimacy is contested by Hasina, her party, and India (which has not extradited her); entry reports the tribunal outcome factually without endorsing either the trial process or critics' objections. India's refusal to return Hasina — Bangladesh's foreign ministry has demanded extradition; India has not complied as of cluster end; AJ's Nov 18 feature analyses why India is unlikely to return her, captured per its framing. Leaked audio (Jul 24, 2025) — Hasina's representatives have disputed authenticity of the leaked recording; entry reports what AJ reported on Jul 24. Public figures named: Hasina, Waker-uz-Zaman (Bangladesh Army Chief, public official). No private individuals named. The 2024 uprising activist whose killing triggered Dec 19, 2025 unrest is publicly identified in reporting but not named in the entry per rule 6.

Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across CNN's Hasina coverage (per user instruction). Same scoping note as t1: focus is tight on Hasina personally and legal accountability arc. Contributing articles from user-provided list: https://edition.cnn.com/2024/08/05/asia/bangladesh-prime-minister-residence-stormed-intl ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/06/asia/bangladesh-protests-hasina-resignation-explainer-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/12/asia/bangladesh-quota-protests-student-martyr-intl-hnk-dst (Aug 12 mural protests) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/14/asia/murder-probe-sheikh-hasina-intl-hnk (first murder case) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/10/17/asia/bangladesh-arrest-warrant-sheikh-hasina-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/12/31/asia/bangladesh-march-hasina-student-protests-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/02/05/asia/bangladesh-protest-hasina-house-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/02/12/asia/un-rights-office-bangladesh-protests-crackdown-intl-latam ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/05/13/asia/bangladesh-sheikh-hasina-awami-party-intl-hnk ; https://edition.cnn.com/2025/07/02/asia/bangladesh-pm-hasina-sentencing-jail-intl-hnk (Jul 2 sentencing) ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/07/10/asia/bangladesh-pm-hasina-indicted-protesters-killed-intl-hnk (Jul 10 indictment) ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/11/17/asia/bangladesh-sheikh-hasina-verdict-intl-hnk (Nov 17 verdict) ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/11/21/asia/bangladesh-india-sheikh-hasina-death-sentence-intl-hnk-dst ; https://edition.cnn.com/2025/12/01/asia/bangladesh-corruption-trial-tulip-siddiq-sheikh-hasina-intl-hnk (Dec 1 Tulip Siddiq corruption trial). Article from non-approved outlet excluded: https://www.ndtv.com/world-news/from-student-uprising-to-death-verdict-a-timeline-of-sheikh-hasinas-fall-9651738 (NDTV not on approved outlet list). Date approximations per rule 4: Two same-date entries on Aug 5, 2024 preserved as distinct events (Hasina's flight, residence storming/looting). Two same-date entries on Aug 6, 2024 preserved (parliament dissolution and CNN explainer publication on the broader resignation arc) — these are arguably blendable, but the parliament dissolution was a discrete state action while CNN's explainer is a structured editorial framing of the resignation; both retained as distinct. Aug 14 (first murder case opened) — pegged to CNN publication date which reported the murder probe; the first criminal complaint against Hasina was filed by a victim's family in mid-August, and CNN's Aug 14 coverage anchors the public reporting. Jul 2, 2025 sentencing — CNN's URL slug indicates a sentencing; entry phrases it as 'imprisonment in absentia' generically since the specific charge (likely contempt or one of the initial cases) is reported variously; phrasing kept neutral. The Nov 17 conviction explicitly noted as 'in absentia' for accuracy. Source disagreements: Conviction legitimacy — the trial took place at the International Crimes Tribunal, which has been historically contested by some legal observers and by Hasina's party; entry reports the outcome factually without endorsing or rejecting the trial's process. India's refusal to extradite — Bangladesh demanded India hand Hasina over (Nov 21 entry); India has not complied; this is captured as Bangladesh's demand rather than as a fait accompli. Tulip Siddiq (Dec 1, 2025) — Hasina's niece and a sitting UK Labour MP; she resigned as UK Treasury minister in January 2025 over the same corruption allegations; the entry names her as she is a sitting public official and the corruption trial concerns her in named legal proceedings (rule 6 public figure exception). Mujibur Rahman (Feb 5, 2025) — Hasina's father, founding leader of Bangladesh (deceased 1975), public historical figure. Public figures named: Hasina, Tulip Siddiq, Mujibur Rahman — all public officials/figures of record. No private individuals named.
